definition: Whether the source explicitly compares wages or compensation to peer jurisdictions or peer units
why_it_matters: This is the main candidate ratchet mechanism the project wants to measure

definition: Whether the source shows formal impasse-resolution institutions such as JLMC, interest arbitration, mediation after impasse, or stipulated awards shaping settlement
why_it_matters: Safety wage-setting may differ because stronger backstop institutions shape outcomes

definition: Whether the source explains why wages changed rather than only listing terms
why_it_matters: Reasoning-rich documents are much more informative than bare agreements

definition: Whether the source names specific comparator cities, towns, or districts
why_it_matters: Named peers are stronger mechanism evidence than generic market language

definition: Whether the source is a clean public final document that could later move into the causal pipeline
why_it_matters: Useful leads are not always ingestion-ready, so this separates calibration value from corpus readiness
